documents.content: Production deploys run through deploy.sh which merges origin/develop, reloads PHP-FPM with SIGUSR2 and clears OPcache. Never restart the app container directly because Horizon needs a 300 second drain window.

To roll back a failed production deploy, check out the previous release tag and re-run the deploy script. Database migrations are not rolled back automatically; use the down methods manually after verifying the schema state.
One platform credit equals 0.001 USD. Budget reservations apply a 1.5x multiplier on the estimated cost and are settled after the LLM call completes with actual token usage.
When a team exceeds its credit budget, the PauseOnBudgetExceeded listener automatically pauses running experiments. Resuming requires topping up credits or raising the budget cap on the project.
The platform uses six Redis queues: critical, ai-calls, experiments, outbound, metrics and default. Horizon supervisors auto-scale ai-calls between two and fifteen workers with a 120 second timeout.
Redis database 0 stores queues, database 1 stores the cache and database 2 stores locks. The predis client is required; phpredis is not supported in this deployment.

Experiments move through twenty states including Draft, Scoring, Planning, Building, AwaitingApproval, Executing, CollectingMetrics, Evaluating and Completed. Any active state can be Paused or Killed.
A failed experiment stage can be retried from a specific step. RetryFromStep performs a graph-aware breadth-first reset that clears only the target step and its downstream dependents.
All domain models use the BelongsToTeam trait with the TeamScope global scope for tenant isolation. PostgreSQL row-level security adds a second enforcement layer below Eloquent.
Sanctum API tokens are scoped to a single team by the ScopeTokenToTeam middleware. Switching teams invalidates previously issued tokens for the old team context.
The semantic cache stores LLM responses with pgvector embeddings and serves a cached answer when cosine similarity to a previous prompt exceeds 0.92. The cache is shared across teams by design.
Each LLM provider has a circuit breaker that opens after five consecutive failures and attempts recovery after sixty seconds in half-open state before fully closing again.
